Liverpool Cancer Drug Interactions Database: Anticoagulants (6) + Antidiabetic agents (5) + Antibiotics (15) + Antifungals (4) + Antihistamines (3) + Antihypertensives (12) + Anti-inflammatory agents (5) + Antipsychotics (4) + Benzodiazepines (8) + Cardiovascular agents (10) + Corticosteroids (6) + Gastrointestinal agents (5) + Hormonal agents (7) + Immunosuppressants (2) + Opioid analgesics (6) + Psychotropic medications (8) + Statins (4) + Miscellaneous agents (25) = 135 total

pi_items.verified_value: Real drug interaction data from Liverpool Drug Interactions Group (joint project: Radboud UMC Nijmegen and University of Liverpool) with evidence grading from very low to high quality
pi_items.source: Cancer iChart app documentation and evidence_docs/ibrutinib_comedication_contraindication_screen_evidence.json which states 'App contains real drug interaction data from Liverpool Drug Interactions Group'

pi_items.verified_value: Database last updated June 2022; original website taken offline September 2023; archived version available through University of Liverpool, with service offline scheduled for 31 January 2025
pi_items.source: cancer-druginteractions.org archived homepage and multiple search results from University of Liverpool announcements

pi_items.verified_value: Oral prodrug of 5-fluorouracil (5-FU); FDA-approved anticancer cytotoxic agent
pi_items.source: PubMed Central articles (PMC3097797, PMC2409640), WebMD, Mayo Clinic, NEJM studies on capecitabine efficacy and safety

privileged_info_summary: Capecitabine is a real FDA-approved oral cancer chemotherapy drug (prodrug of 5-FU) used for colorectal, breast, and gastric cancer treatment. It is included in the Liverpool Cancer Drug Interactions Database, a real, publicly-accessible resource developed by Radboud UMC (Nijmegen) and the University of Liverpool. For Capecitabine, the database documents approximately 18 therapeutic drug categories with approximately 135 total individual drug interactions (including anticoagulants with 6 drugs, antibiotics with 15 drugs, antihypertensives with 12 drugs, cardiovascular agents with 10 drugs, benzodiazepines with 8 drugs, and many others). The database was last updated in June 2022 and contains evidence-graded clinical recommendations. The Cancer iChart app is an offline mobile application (iOS/Android) that downloads this real interaction database, not synthetic data. The task metadata minimums (min_categories: 3, min_total_drugs: 10) are easily satisfied by the actual database contents.
